mission.title: Install and Configure Openyurt on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenYurt extends your native Kubernetes to edge environments, enabling seamless management of edge applications as if they were running in the cloud. Installing OpenYurt allows you to address challenges in cloud-edge orchestration, such as unreliable networking and edge device management.

steps.title: Install OpenYurt control plane via Helm
steps.description:
Add the OpenYurt Helm repository and install:
```bash
helm repo add openyurt https://openyurtio.github.io/openyurt-helm
helm repo update
kubectl create namespace openyurt-system
helm install yurt-manager openyurt/yurt-manager --namespace openyurt-system --version 1.6.0
```

steps.title: Install YurtHub on edge nodes
steps.description:
Label nodes as edge nodes and install YurtHub:
```bash
kubectl label node <edge-node-name> openyurt.io/is-edge-worker=true
```
YurtHub is automatically deployed to labeled edge nodes by the operator.

steps.title: Create a NodePool
steps.description:
Organize edge nodes into pools:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: apps.openyurt.io/v1beta1
kind: NodePool
metadata:
  name: edge-pool
spec:
  type: Edge
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n openyurt-system
kubectl get nodepools
kubectl get nodes -l openyurt.io/is-edge-worker=true
```

resolution.summary: OpenYurt is installed with the yurt-manager control plane and can manage edge nodes with autonomous operation.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Helm release
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall yurt-manager -n openyurt-system
kubectl delete namespace openyurt-system
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade via Helm
upgrade.description:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade yurt-manager openyurt/yurt-manager --namespace openyurt-system
```

troubleshooting.title: YurtHub not connecting to API server
troubleshooting.description:
Check YurtHub logs on the edge node:
```bash
kubectl logs -l k8s-app=yurt-hub -n kube-system --context <edge-node>
```

troubleshooting.title: Edge node shows NotReady
troubleshooting.description: YurtHub enables edge autonomy — the node may show NotReady if disconnected from the cloud but workloads continue running.

troubleshooting.title: NodePool not scheduling workloads
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure nodes are labeled and joined to the pool:
```bash
kubectl describe nodepool edge-pool
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed on your local machine.
